mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 5-day forward returns following 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS are not positive in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals accelerating labor market deterioration that depresses consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes in which UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in 3 months produce negative 20-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of cases over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean return during triggers is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales reflect broad-based demand recovery that boosts corporate revenue expectations; this joint acceleration produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days only when both series rise together.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint trigger of INDPRO and RSAFS both rising month-over-month does not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of such instances, or if an interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over the main effects in a rolling 3-year regression (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-interest-rate policy regime, rising consumer prices reflect reflation rather than tightening risk, reducing real debt burdens and boosting nominal earnings expectations, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If months with FEDFUNDS below 1% and rising CPIAUCSL fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the sign of the CPIAUCSL effect flips negative within this regime over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals improving factory utilization and operating leverage, which directly expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts exceeding 5% signals strengthening residential investment and construction employment, which raises aggregate demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST rising more than 5% over 3 months fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls signal contracting household income and consumption capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors discount lower aggregate demand.
failure_condition: If consecutive monthly declines in PAYEMS are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP reflects accelerating aggregate output that lifts corporate sales and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If quarter-over-quarter increases in GDPC1 coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
